threat_model: Trusted identity headers can be spoofed by clients if Mullu accepts them without an upstream gateway that strips client-supplied values and injects verified OIDC or mTLS identity claims. The mitigation is a fail-closed, evidence-backed assessment that treats headers as untrusted unless the gateway path proves strip, injection, verifier, freshness or certificate-chain, bypass protection, and receipt refs.

required_checks.check: IDOR and cross-tenant spoofing check through missing trusted-header gateway evidence
policy bypass test for disabled default and complete evidence-only admission
receipt integrity test requiring evidence refs before trusted headers are accepted
audit visibility check through documented blocker codes and SDLC evidence refs

mitigations: disable trusted identity headers by default
require a complete OIDC or mTLS verifier path
require rollback or bypass protection evidence
